html:
HARN-R.BK is not the rights-offering recap story in the draft. The better thesis is cleaner: a THB 1.15bn market-cap Thai microcap engineering supplier with net cash, a 6% dividend yield, a flat ignored chart, and real optionality to Thailand’s data-center, power, petrochemical, refrigeration, carbon, and medical 3D-printing buildout.

At THB 1.96, HARN trades at about 13.7x trailing earnings, 0.82x book, and 6.0x EV/EBITDA on the latest SET factsheet. That screen is only moderately cheap. The variant view is that 2025 was an unexciting base year, not normalized earnings power. Revenue fell 4.8% to THB 1.21bn, but gross margin improved to 29.0%, operating cash flow jumped to THB 209m, and year-end cash plus short-term investments reached about THB 537m against roughly THB 101m of debt. Even after the THB 0.12/share dividend payable on May 22, the company remains overcapitalized for its current size.

The market still labels HARN as a sleepy fire-protection and digital-printing distributor. The business is more interesting than that. Fire protection is 45% of revenue, digital printing 31%, refrigeration 17%, with smaller air-conditioning, sanitary, IoT, and new medical 3D-printing pieces. The company’s own 2026 plan explicitly targets power plants, petrochemicals, and data centers, while expanding design, installation, and inspection services. Those are not buzzwords pasted onto an old business. HARN sells clean-agent fire suppression systems for data centers, computer rooms, server rooms, control rooms, and electrical rooms, and its products are typically UL/FM/VdS/BS/TIS/EN certified. In hyperscale infrastructure, these are mandatory systems, not discretionary upgrades.

Thailand is the reason the setup has torque. BOI approvals and applications show a step-change in data-center activity: 36 applications worth THB 728bn in 2025, another seven projects worth about THB 96bn approved in early 2026, and large follow-on approvals led by TikTok and Bridge Data Centres. True IDC has broken ground on a 250MW EEC campus, Microsoft is committing more than $1bn to Thailand cloud and AI infrastructure during 2026-2028, and Bangkok planned data-center capacity has moved sharply higher. HARN does not need to win the data-center cycle. A few specialized fire-safety, cooling, inspection, or service packages can matter because the company’s entire market cap is only about THB 1.15bn.

The 2028 Jump+ target is the anchor: EBITDA of THB 211m, up 57% from THB 134m in 2025. If HARN reaches that, today’s stock is around 3.7x post-dividend EV/2028 EBITDA before any cash build, and roughly 8-9x likely 2028 net income. That is too low for a net-cash, dividend-paying engineering solutions company if the business mix moves toward complex infrastructure rather than contractor-driven commodity supply.

The near-term underwriting is more modest. Backlog was THB 464m at year-end 2025, up from THB 379m at 9M25 and equal to about 38% of 2025 revenue. This is not a fully de-risked revenue explosion, but it is enough visibility to support recovery. If 2026 revenue grows 10-15% to THB 1.33-1.39bn and net margin normalizes to 7-7.5% as credit costs and one-time import-duty friction fade, net profit can reach THB 95-105m, or THB 0.16-0.18/share. That puts the stock at 11-12x 2026 earnings, but only about 7-8x earnings after net cash. The 6% dividend yield pays investors while they wait.

The bear case is real: HARN has low ROE, thin liquidity, high customer credit exposure, and a history of slow growth. Expected credit losses rose in 2025, construction-sector customers remain a risk, and the dividend payout is high. Management’s carbon subsidiary and medical 3D-printing venture could become distractions if they consume capital before proving commercial demand. The biggest thesis breaker would be backlog stagnation plus another year of ECL leakage, proving HARN is just a low-return distributor with fashionable strategy language.

But the asymmetry is still attractive because the stock already prices in dullness. It trades below book, near tangible book, with net cash, current ratio above 4x, no meaningful leverage, and a recurring dividend. The upside comes from a small revenue mix shift: more data-center fire protection, more high-spec refrigeration and IoT monitoring, more inspection/service work, and possibly one JV/M&A deal that gives the market a visible new growth leg.

Targets from THB 1.96 current price:

Bear: THB 1.45, down 26%. Revenue stays flat, ECL remains elevated, ROE stays around 5-6%, and the market values HARN at 0.8x tangible book.

Base: THB 2.70, up 38%. 2026-2027 revenue compounds around low-teens, net profit reaches THB 110-120m, EBITDA approaches THB 170-180m, and the market values the business at 6.5x EV/EBITDA plus net cash.

Bull: THB 4.00, up 104%. HARN proves the Jump+ plan, reaches roughly THB 211m EBITDA by 2028, converts data-center and industrial demand into higher-margin engineering work, and rerates to about 9x EV/EBITDA or 16x earnings.
